meaning: n.鍏珛闊充箰瀛︽牎,鑹烘湳瀛︽牎
examples: Are you a graduate of a Conservatoire?
At the age of 11, she won first prize at the Paris Conservatoire.
It will also be the 200th anniversary of the opening of the Prague Conservatoire.
The British Council in Egypt called the Cairo Conservatoire, who suggested Marcellino Safwat, a 17-year-old cellist with ambitions to become a conductor.
Instead, he studied organ and composition at the Paris Conservatoire, where his reputation fostered his meeting and subsequent close friendship with Franz Liszt.
